g.data-narration: Welcome to our exploration of decoders, essential circuits that convert binary information into unique output signals.
A decoder translates binary information from n input lines to maximum 2^n unique output lines, activating only one output at a time.
g.text: One AND ONLY ONE output is

g.data-narration: Decoders include an Enable input that controls when the decoder functions, crucial for cascading multiple decoders together.
g.text: When E=1: Decoder functions normally - outputs reflect input combination
When E=0: All outputs forced to 0 (disabled) - regardless of inputs
Enable allows cascading smaller decoders to build larger ones

g.data-narration: Let us examine a 2-to-4 decoder with inputs E, A1, A0 and outputs Y0 through Y3, demonstrating the core decoder principle.
g.text: Inputs: E (Enable), A1, A0 (2-bit binary) | Outputs: Y0, Y1, Y2, Y3

g.data-narration: The truth table shows decoder behavior: when enabled, exactly one output corresponds to the binary input decimal value.
Each output is a minterm - a unique product of the inputs. Y0 activates when E=1 and binary input equals decimal 0.
g.text: Each output Y is a minterm - a unique product (AND) of inputs:

g.data-narration: The decoder circuit uses NOT gates to generate complements and AND gates for each minterm output activation.
We can build a 3-to-8 decoder by cascading two 2-to-4 decoders, using the third input A2 as enable control.
g.text: Hierarchical design: Use A2 as enable signal to select which 2-to-4 decoder is active
When A2=0: First decoder enabled (outputs Y0-Y3)
When A2=1: Second decoder enabled (outputs Y4-Y7)

g.data-narration: A decoder generates all minterms of input variables, making it a powerful tool for implementing any Boolean function.
g.text: An n-to-2^n decoder generates ALL 2^n minterms of n input variables.
This makes decoders perfect for implementing combinational logic without manual simplification!

g.data-narration: Example: Implement F(A,B,C) = Σm(1,3,5,6) using a 3-to-8 decoder - simply connect outputs Y1,Y3,Y5,Y6 to an OR gate.
Decoders can have active-high outputs (1 when selected) or active-low outputs (0 when selected), common in real chips like 74x138.
Decoders are essential for memory address selection, instruction decoding in CPUs, and data routing - fundamental to all digital systems.
